mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 740d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 740d engine typically costs £2,500–£5,500 supply only depending on generation. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A blown engine on a premium BMW typically costs £9,000–£13,000 to replace. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends entirely on which engine generation your 740d has. The E65's M67 V8 is a rare and complex engine — rewarding when sorted but expensive to maintain. The N57 (F01/G11) is described as 'brilliant' with seamless torque delivery. The B57 (G11 LCI) introduced 'better timing chain durability' and 48V mild-hybrid technology. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 740d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Turbocharger and boost pressure issues (power loss, whistling, increased fuel consumption), timing chain wear on N57 engines, EGR and DPF clogging from short urban journeys, and high-pressure injection pump failure on the M67 V8.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 740d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £5,000–£15,000 740d, a £3,500–£5,500 recon is 25–110% of the car's value. For a clean post-2020 B57 example, replacement is almost always worth it given the engine's advanced design and strong retained values. For older E65 M67 examples, evaluate the car's overall condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 740d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E65 (2002–2005) has M67D39 (3.9L V8), F01 (2009–2012) has N57D30 (3.0L straight-six), G11 (2015–2020) has N57D30B (3.0L straight-six), G11 LCI (2020–2022) has B57D30B (3.0L straight-six with MHEV). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,200–£2,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£4,000–£7,000 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The B57 engine introduced 'better timing chain durability' to address this issue.

eraMap.title: BMW 740d — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 3.9L V8 diesel — rare and complex. 258 hp, 600 Nm. High-pressure injection pump and turbo issues documented
3.0L straight-six twin-turbo — 306 hp, 600 Nm. Refined and responsive, timing chain more reliable than N47
3.0L straight-six twin-turbo — 320 hp, 680 Nm. Improved over earlier N57, better chain durability
3.0L straight-six twin-turbo with 48V MHEV — 340 hp, 700 Nm. B57 successor with improved timing chain durability

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 740d engine typically takes 14–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The E65's V8 and the G11's complex twin-turbo layout add significant labour time.

cta.label: → Compare 740d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Turbocharger & Boost Pressure Issues (All Generations)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, power loss, whistling or hissing noise during acceleration, stalling at idle, increased fuel consumption
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,500 (turbocharger repair or replacement; wastegate valve repair)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you hear whistling or experience power loss

problems.recommendation: Boost pressure issues are a common complaint on the 740d across all generations. Symptoms include an engine light, power loss, a whistling noise during acceleration, stalling at idle, and increased fuel consumption. One owner reported that a 2016 740d experienced power loss, rough running, hissing, and increased fuel consumption. In some cases, the problem is a faulty wastegate valve. If you experience these symptoms, have the turbo system inspected immediately — a £800–£2,500 repair is far cheaper than the engine damage that can follow if ignored.

failureLink.label: Read the full turbo failure guide

problems.title: Timing Chain Wear (N57, F01 & Early G11)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the engine, engine warning light, loss of power
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — more common on earlier N57 engines

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: While the N57 is more reliable than the infamous N47 diesel, timing chain wear remains a documented issue. BMW timing chains have snapped on a range of models, most commonly affecting the N47 diesel but also present on N57 engines. The B57 engine introduced "better timing chain durability" to counter this. At the first sign of cold-start rattling, get the chain inspected immediately. A £1,200–£2,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £4,000–£7,000 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/n57-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: EGR & DPF Issues (All Generations, Especially B57)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, reduced performance, increased fuel consumption, DPF warning light, "Drivetrain" warning message
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles — short urban journeys accelerate the issue
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 (forced DPF regeneration / EGR cleaning) / £1,000–£2,500 (DPF or EGR replacement)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: The B57 engine introduced improvements to the DPF and EGR systems "to counter common emission related challenges". However, like all modern diesels, the 740d is sensitive to short urban journeys that prevent the engine from reaching optimal temperatures for regeneration. If you primarily drive short distances, factor in regular motorway runs to allow passive DPF regenerations. Ignoring DPF warnings can lead to clogged filters, requiring expensive replacement. The EGR cooler recall affects many BMW diesel engines — verify recall completion before buying any 740d.

failureLink.label: Read the full DPF clogging guide

problems.title: High-Pressure Injection Pump Failure (M67 V8)
problems.symptoms: Hard starting, rough idle, loss of power, engine stalling, fuel in oil

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you experience hard starting or rough idle

problems.recommendation: The M67 V8 diesel in the E65 740d is a rare and complex engine. The high-pressure injection pump is a known weak point, with failures leading to fuel system contamination and potentially catastrophic engine damage. Parts availability for the M67 is limited, making repairs more expensive. If you're considering an E65 740d, factor in the cost of a preventative injection pump inspection or replacement. This is a specialist-only job.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 740d? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2020 B57-engined car (48V MHEV, improved timing chain durability)
* Documented timing chain history on any N57-engined car
* Full service history with BMW Longlife-04 oil specification
avoidIf: * N57-engined car with cold-start rattle and no chain history — this is a ticking time bomb
* E65 M67 V8 with no documented injection pump service — parts are rare and expensive
* Poor service history — diesel BMWs are unforgiving of neglect
* DPF warning lights with no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 740d badge spans four very different diesel engines. The E65's M67 V8 is a rare and complex beast — rewarding when sorted but expensive to maintain. The F01/G11 N57 straight-six is widely regarded as a brilliant engine — "the third-generation twin-turbo unit is, in a word, brilliant". The G11 LCI's B57 introduced mild-hybrid technology and "better timing chain durability", making it the cleanest slate of any 740d generation. Buy a post-2020 B57 if you want the most reliable option. Buy on documented history, not on generation alone.

cta.label: → Compare 740d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 740d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking (timing chain warning — this is critical)
* Turbo Health — Listen for whistling or hissing under acceleration; check for power loss
* Service History — Including which engine era (M67, N57 or B57) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Timing Chain History — Has the chain been replaced? N57 engines need this documented
* DPF Health — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys are a red flag
* EGR Recall — Verify EGR cooler recall completion — affects many BMW diesel engines
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for boost, timing or emission-related codes
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
